steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of the text's overall structure. Map the three sentences: (1) defines community science; (2) lists three benefits — conservation, youth interest, more data; (3) names a specific study by Merrill that used this approach.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'introduces the topic of a study, describes its importance, then presents its results': The text never presents Merrill's results — it only mentions the study exists. ✗
  • Choice B — 'argues for a new approach, comments on public opinion, then describes its application': The text doesn't argue for a new approach or comment on public opinion. ✗
  • Choice C — 'identifies a research approach, lists benefits, then mentions a study that used it': Sentence 1 identifies the approach, sentence 2 lists benefits, sentence 3 mentions a study using it. Exact match. ✓
  • Choice D — 'describes the development of a collaboration type, shows its use in a field, then suggests future projects': The text describes no historical development and suggests no future projects. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
